description_long: This is a large, single-family house set on a sloped terrain, with a prominent dark gable roof and a wide, covered porch or deck area extending along the front. The structure features multiple rooflines and overhangs, giving it a layered and substantial appearance. The exterior walls appear to be finished in a neutral tone, possibly stucco or painted wood, with large windows distributed across the facade for ample natural light. The house is elevated above the ground on one side due to the slope, with visible support columns beneath the porch. There is a driveway or paved area adjacent to the house, likely for vehicle access, and the surrounding landscape is primarily grass. The overall design suggests a blend of Craftsman and modern mountain lodge styles, suitable for suburban or semi-rural neighborhoods.
description: A large, gable-roofed house with a wide covered porch, set on a grassy slope with a driveway and multiple large windows. The design blends Craftsman and modern lodge elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the house elevated on a slope, with the porch and support columns visible beneath the main structure.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the wide covered porch, main entryway, and the long grassy approach leading up to the house.
description_view_2: Opposite side view showing the driveway or paved area adjacent to the house and the continuation of the sloped terrain.
description_view_3: Rear view displaying the back of the house, the driveway area, and the slope descending away from the structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large windows and grouped panes
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway features a covered porch with steps, prominent columns, and a wide overhang.
